findings: DOABILITY: Step 10 (image_fill_area to "place the white-bg hero onto a solid pure-#FFFFFF square 1:1 canvas... extends the white field to a square") is not connector-doable: image_fill_area is a SOLID-color fill of a selection WITHIN the existing canvas — it cannot extend/grow the canvas or composite an image onto a larger square canvas. Squaring a non-square image requires canvas padding/extension, which only the banned image_generative_expand does. The reverify explicitly swapped the banned generative_expand for image_fill_area, but image_fill_area physically cannot produce a square 2048x2048 canvas from the non-square source. All other 15 steps are real, doable [C] Adobe actions.  |  PRESENTATION: Spec is complete and well-formed (one deliverable set, full_brief, inputs with realism_notes, outputs with specs, coherent chain, reverify present). Nits: (1) `vertical` is mislabeled "Nonprofit, Religious & Community" for a firearms/optics ecom store; (2) the scope_hero_square_2048 output spec still says "generative-expanded to a square canvas" — stale wording left over from the pre-rework version, which also flags the underlying doability gap in step 10.  |  TOP: Step 10 uses image_fill_area to turn a non-square white-bg hero into a square 1:1 2048px canvas, but image_fill_area only solid-fills a selection within the existing canvas and cannot extend/pad the canvas — the one tool that squares by extension (image_generative_expand) is banned and was deliberately removed, so this step cannot produce its stated square-hero deliverable.

DOABILITY: Step 16 (banner) uses image_fill_area to "place the graded ~2400x2400 square master on a solid white 16:9 2560x1440 canvas, bracelet offset, with text-safe negative space." image_fill_area is solid-color fill of a selection WITHIN an existing image — explicitly "NOT a compositor" — and cannot enlarge/re-shape a square into a wider-than-source canvas with the subject offset and added white space. That is canvas extension = image_generative_expand (banned) or local compositing. The reverify note itself admits step 16 was an image_generative_expand outpaint "replaced by image_fill_area" — a mislabel, not a real fix. Its output spec (2560x1440, wider than the 2400 source) is unachievable with fill alone. All other 20 steps are valid, correctly-ordered headless connector actions.  |  PRESENTATION: Otherwise complete and well-formed: inputs with realism_notes, outputs with specs, hub-and-spoke workflow chain, reverify present, counts reconciled (21==21, 17==17). Weakness is the mislabeled step 16, whose output spec demands a 2560x1440 canvas wider than the ~2400px square source — impossible via image_fill_area.  |  TOP: Step 16 banner: image_fill_area cannot expand a square master onto a wider 16:9 canvas with offset subject + white negative space — that is canvas extension (image_generative_expand / banned) or compositing, mislabeled as a solid fill; the 2560x1440 output exceeds the source width and is not connector-doable.

IP/NAMING: The full_brief and one_line_ask explicitly name three real, living, world-famous footballers — Lionel Messi, Cristiano Ronaldo, and Kylian Mbappé — as the depicted subjects of a commercial poster. These are real people with right-of-publicity/likeness protection, so the brief as delivered commissions grading a poster built around named celebrity likenesses. The grounding_note and input gen_prompts try to launder this ("faces generic, not actual celebrities", no real flags/logos, fictional Continental Cup), and the actual generated assets are generic/unbranded — but the brief text the agent receives still names the three real players. This is the same category as using a real person's name/likeness; it should not be flagged only if it were a fictional brand, and here real individuals are named.  |  PRESENTATION: Spec is otherwise very complete (one deliverable set, full_brief, inputs with realism_notes, outputs with specs, coherent output→input spine, reverify present). But the chaining_note (line 321) is stale: it still narrates "generative_expand to A2 bleed(17)" even though the workflow array and reverify correctly use image_crop_to_bounds at step 17 — a lingering inconsistency from the fix that should be reconciled. distinct_adobe_tools=18 vs tools_used array of 17 names (asset_initialize/finalize count separately in the workflow) is a minor bookkeeping mismatch.  |  TOP: IP: the full_brief/one_line_ask name three real living celebrities (Messi, Ronaldo, Mbappé) as poster subjects — a right-of-publicity/likeness problem — despite the reframing note claiming generic faces; the delivered brief still commissions their named likenesses.

IP/NAMING: The client is NOT fictional. The spec's own grounding_note states the brand is 'VERBATIM' from a real Upwork brief ('cand_52'): 'John Paul the Great Academy, a Catholic classical school in Lafayette, Louisiana' — a specific, named, geographically-pinned real religious institution whose name also invokes a real historical/religious figure (Pope John Paul II, 'John Paul the Great'). Real 'John Paul the Great' Catholic schools/academies exist. The benchmark requires the CLIENT to be an invented/fictional brand; using a verbatim real school (name + denomination + city + founding year 2006 + anniversary) violates that rule and carries real-org/trademark and sensitivity risk. Delivery-platform allowances (Upwork/Firefly) do not apply — the flagged entity is the client itself.  |  DOABILITY: No banned-capability violation (from-scratch folk-art art is correctly supplied as an approved input, not generated; image_fill_area is solid white preset fill; render_vector only exports the authored separations.ai; no text-to-image/gen-fill/object-removal/upscaling). But two connector-accuracy slips, both self-noted in reverify: step 7 image_vectorize has no maskURI param so the lettering_mask input is inert (it would trace the whole raster, not just lettering — an intermediate masked-raster step is needed); step 16 claims one render emits PDF+PNG+SVG when render_vector emits one media type per call (3 calls). Also loose print semantics: whole-layer gaussian blur isn't a true dot choke and tint/overlay on one shared field yields colored proofs, not independently-registering spot channels. Feasible as a headless job but not literally a press-ready 5-screen sep pack.  |  TOP: IP: client 'John Paul the Great Academy' is a REAL, named, verbatim religious institution (Lafayette, LA), not a fictional invented brand — violates the benchmark's core fictional-client requirement.

DOABILITY: Step 8 (image_fill_area) cannot do what it claims: it fills a masked region of an EXISTING image with solid color within the current canvas — it cannot enlarge/extend a 1024x1024 frame onto a new 1080x1920 canvas or composite the frame onto a larger solid ground. Going square→vertical canvas is a canvas-extension op the connector only offers via image_generative_expand (BANNED). No non-generative solid-fill path achieves this. Worse, the spec still carries live references to the banned capability the reverify claims was removed: output spec line 72 says the cover is "outpainted top+bottom to 9:16"; chaining_note (line 270) says "→ generative_expand to 9:16 (8)"; difficulty_rationale (line 271) says "The one generative step is image_generative_expand (outpaint) only". These contradict the patched workflow and reference a banned tool. All other steps (remove_bg, vectorize, preset/temp/single-color-sat grade, stock license+add_grain, media_enhance_speech [A], video_resize same-length [A], font_recommend, firefly board) are real, doable connector actions.  |  PRESENTATION: Well-structured overall: one deliverable kit, full_brief, inputs with realism_notes, outputs with specs, mostly coherent chain, reverify present. But: (1) output spec, chaining_note, and difficulty_rationale still describe outpaint / image_generative_expand at step 8, contradicting the image_fill_area used in connector_workflow — stale, uncleaned text referencing a banned capability; (2) step 8's image_fill_area note describes a canvas-extension/placement it cannot perform; (3) brand naming drift ("RoadWise" vs "driving_sense"/"drivingsense" filenames).  |  TOP: Cover/end-card step 8 is not connector-doable — image_fill_area cannot extend a 1024x1024 frame onto a 1080x1920 canvas (that needs the banned image_generative_expand), and the output spec/chaining_note/difficulty_rationale still reference outpaint/generative_expand, a banned capability.

IP/NAMING: "Beacon Mutual Insurance Group" is confusingly close to a REAL company: Beacon Mutual Insurance Company is an actual, well-known Rhode Island insurance carrier (workers' comp). The core mark "Beacon Mutual Insurance" is nearly identical and in the exact same industry (insurance); adding "Group" and switching the line to personal auto does not clear the collision. Should be renamed to a clearly invented, non-colliding brand.  |  TOP: Client brand "Beacon Mutual Insurance Group" collides with the real Beacon Mutual Insurance Company (a RI insurance carrier) — same industry, near-identical name; must be renamed to a clearly fictional brand.

That contradicts the corrected non-generative workflow and should be reworded to "solid pure-white field placement." reverify.notes also flags a pre-existing render_layout export-only nit left as-is.  |  TOP: Stale "outpainted/generative_expand" wording in the output spec and full_brief contradicts the corrected image_fill_area (solid-fill) step 12 — cosmetic doc drift to reword, not a banned-capability or IP blocker.

Everything else is clean and doable.

DOABILITY: Step 9 (image_fill_area) is assigned a canvas-EXTENSION job it cannot do: the note says it "places the popped hero on a solid near-black charcoal brand canvas at the wider 16:9 target" with new dark negative space on the LEFT. image_fill_area is solid-color fill of an existing region only (select→[invert]→fill) — it cannot enlarge the frame from the 3:2 source to a wider 16:9 or add new left-side space. This is the leftover 1:1 swap of the removed banned image_generative_expand; the tool is allowed but mis-tasked. Workaroundable (crop_and_resize in step 10 can reach 16:9 with the subject offset so the dark surround already reads as left negative space), so not a hard blocker. All other 21 steps are real, headless [C] Adobe actions with no banned capability (no gen-fill/outpaint/upscale/text-to-image/object-removal-reconstruction).  |  PRESENTATION: Structure is complete (inputs with realism_notes, outputs with specs, chained workflow, reverify present). These references contradict the real connector_workflow and should be updated to match.  |  TOP: Step 9 image_fill_area is mis-tasked with a canvas-extension/reframe (3:2 → wider 16:9 with new left negative space) that solid-fill cannot do — leftover from the removed image_generative_expand swap; the deliverable is still reachable via crop_and_resize, but the step note plus the stale chaining_note/difficulty_rationale (which still say "generative_expand"/"outpaint") need fixing.

Also note the 16:9 landscape 2560x1440 hero is derived purely by cropping a 4000x5000 portrait, which is a heavy reframe that relies heavily on the "empty backdrop room" and may leave little vertical headroom around the subject.  |  TOP: Stale "outpaint/generative_expand" prose in grounding_note, input realism_notes, and difficulty_rationale contradicts the corrected crop-only workflow (executable chain is clean; text needs updating to match).

DOABILITY: No BANNED capability remains (prior generative_expand was swapped out; vectorize/halftone/remove_bg/solid-fill are all allowed and headless). But two connector-fidelity gaps: (1) Step 14 uses image_fill_area to build a "wide banner / social-header lockup" from a square mark — image_fill_area only fills a masked region of an EXISTING image with solid color; it cannot expand/create a differently-sized canvas or composite a transparent foreground onto a new banner-sized background, so this deliverable is not achievable with the named tool (a true wide-banner would need canvas expansion = banned, or a layout/composite tool). (2) Step 12's single-colour recolor uses the select_subject→invert_selection→fill_area chain that the image_fill_area docs explicitly warn against (transparent-PNG-as-mask = wrong polarity). Also step 11 image_invert_selection is a dead/unused step (spec self-admits).  |  PRESENTATION: Well-formed: verbatim grounding_note + source URL, inputs with realism_notes, outputs with specs, strict chaining_note, reverify present. DOABILITY: Step 10 uses asset_preview_file to pull "only the flagged pages" of the proof PDF into a new subset flagged-pages PDF. Preview tools generate previews of a file; extracting/subsetting specific pages into a new multi-page PDF is a page-selection/split operation the preview tool does not natively perform, so the tool mapping is imperfect. The operation itself is benign and non-generative (no annotations drawn, no new content), so this is a mapping stretch, not a banned capability. All other steps (create folders, upload init/finalize, document_merge_data_layout [T] on client-authored .indd, document_render_layout, document_convert_pdf, asset_preview_file/inline_preview inspect, media_summarize [A], local write/zip) are real connector-doable actions with no banned capability.  |  TOP: Step 10's use of asset_preview_file to extract only the flagged pages into a new subset PDF is an imperfect tool mapping (page-selection/split is not a preview-tool capability); the underlying operation is benign and non-generative, so it is a small fixable nit rather than a task-breaker.
